reasoning_content: We need to determine if 6703 is prime. Let's check divisibility. First, note sqrt(6703) is about 81.9, so we need to test primes up to 83. Check small primes: 2: odd, no. 3: sum digits 6+7+0+3=16, not divisible by 3. 5: doesn't end in 0 or 5. 7: 7*957=6699, remainder 4, so not divisible. 11: alternating sum (6-7+0-3) = -4, not multiple of 11. 13: 13*515=6695, remainder 8; 13*516=6708, too high. 17: 17*394=6698, remainder 5; 17*395=6715. 19: 19*353=6707, remainder -4; 19*352=6688, remainder 15. 23: 23*291=6693, remainder 10; 23*292=6716. 29: 29*231=6699, remainder 4; 29*232=6728. 31: 31*216=6696, remainder 7; 31*217=6727. 37: 37*181=6697, remainder 6; 37*182=6734. 41: 41*163=6683, remainder 20; 41*164=6724. 43: 43*156=6708, too high; 43*155=6665, remainder 38. 47: 47*142=6674, remainder 29; 47*143=6721. 53: 53*126=6678, remainder 25; 53*127=6731. 59: 59*113=6667, remainder 36; 59*114=6726. 61: 61*109=6649, remainder 54; 61*110=6710. 67: 67*100=6700, remainder 3; 67*101=6767. 71: 71*94=6674, remainder 29; 71*95=6745. 73: 73*91=6643, remainder 60; 73*92=6716. 79: 79*84=6636, remainder 67; 79*85=6715. 83: 83*80=6640, remainder 63; 83*81=6723, remainder -20. So none divide evenly. Thus 6703 is prime. Answer: yes.
